findings.title: Hardcoded JWT secret
findings.description: The JWT secret is hardcoded as a string literal. This should be loaded from environment variables to prevent secret exposure in source control.
findings.suggestedFix:
const SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET;
if (!SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET env var required');

findings.title: SQL injection in getUserData
findings.description: User-controlled input is interpolated directly into a SQL query string. This is a classic SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.suggestedFix:
const query = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1';
return db.query(query, [userId]);

findings.title: Admin check uses username instead of role
findings.description: The adminOnly middleware checks user.username === 'admin' instead of checking a proper role field. Any user named 'admin' would get admin access, and actual admins with different usernames would be denied.

findings.title: IDOR: missing authorization on DELETE endpoint
findings.description: Any authenticated user can delete any other user's account. The endpoint only checks authentication but not authorization.
findings.suggestedFix: // Add check: if (req.user.id !== id && !req.user.roles.includes('admin')) return res.status(403)...

findings.title: Missing pagination on user listing endpoint
findings.description: The GET /users endpoint fetches all users without pagination. This will cause performance issues and excessive memory usage at scale.
findings.suggestedFix:
const { page = 1, limit = 20 } = req.query;
// Add LIMIT and OFFSET to query
